refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource allocation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource allocation) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations; verify improvements in equipment operation, task scheduling efficiency, and resource utilization.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput.
FR2: Schedule tasks efficiently to minimize idle time and delays.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to maintain productivity targets.

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation optimization.
DP2: Intelligent task scheduling system integrated with teleoperation.
DP3: Dynamic resource management via Safety Services and Teleoperation coordination.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation optimization.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and teleoperation tasking.
Coordination needed with Safety Services for safe operation.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled but requires interface coordination with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle control algorithms can be effectively integrated with teleoperation commands.
Risk of productivity degradation if coordination with Safety Services is insufficient.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit DP for task scheduling and resource allocation in next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends operational data and status to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands and scheduling inputs from Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety status and alerts with Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of vehicle control DP; low diffusion as control logic is centralized.
Limited containment; mainly interfaces with vehicle control.
Safety logic is separate; some interface coupling.
High-level coordination; minimal direct containment of vehicle control DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module best encapsulates the advanced vehicle control DP with minimal diffusion and clear interface boundaries.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services.
Risk of duplicated control logic if coordination is not well managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and integration protocols to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (advanced vehicle control algorithms) logically satisfies the refined FR of optimizing equipment operation.

diffusion_risks: Interface complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Safety alert coordination may introduce coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment of DP with low diffusion scores.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring vehicle operational metrics and telemetry data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose FRs for task scheduling and resource allocation in next level.
Refine interface definitions to reduce diffusion risks.

decision.justification: The current level decomposition and assignments are sound for the vehicle control aspect, but additional FRs and DPs for task scheduling and resource allocation are needed to fully cover the productivity KPI.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput.
FR2: Schedule tasks efficiently to minimize idle time and delays.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to maintain productivity targets.

revise.why: To decompose the productivity KPI into more granular functional requirements covering all key productivity drivers.

assumptions: Vehicle control algorithms can be integrated with teleoperation and safety modules.
Interfaces will be clearly defined to avoid diffusion.
open_risks: Interface complexity and potential duplication of control logic.
evidence_needed: Performance data from vehicle control algorithms during representative operations.
Interface compliance and integration test results.
